steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The blank completes a phrase describing what researchers were testing: whether false visual memories are caused by people's previous ______ incorrect renditions of images. The word or phrase must describe a plausible causal mechanism — something people experience that could produce false memories.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — compliance with: 'Compliance with' means following rules or orders. You comply with instructions, not images. This doesn't describe a mechanism for false memory formation. ✗
  • Choice B — exposure to: 'Exposure to' means previous contact with or familiarity with something. If people had seen incorrect renditions before, that contact could plausibly produce false memories — this is exactly the hypothesis being tested (and then disproved). ✓
  • Choice C — criteria for: 'Criteria for' means standards used to judge something. This implies people set evaluation standards for incorrect images, which is grammatically awkward and logically irrelevant to causing false memories. ✗
  • Choice D — forfeiture of: 'Forfeiture of' means giving up or surrendering something. The idea that people gave up incorrect images would not logically cause false memories of those images. ✗
B is correct.
